article_title: Anthropic Accidentally Exposes Claude Code's Entire Source Code Through npm Packaging Error, Days After Mythos Leak

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: Anthropic Accidentally Exposes Claude Code's Entire Source Code Through npm Packaging Error, Days After Mythos Leak
content_preview.summary: A misconfigured npm package exposed 512,000 lines of Claude Code's TypeScript source code via a source map file pointing to Anthropic's cloud storage, marking the company's second data exposure in less than a week.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Anthropic accidentally published the complete source code for Claude Code, its AI-powered command-line development tool, through a misconfigured npm package on March 31. A source map file included in the release pointed to a zip archive hosted on Anthropic's Cloudflare R2 storage bucket, exposing approximately 1,900 TypeScript files and over 512,000 lines of unobfuscated code, according to [The Register](https://www.theregister.com/2026/03/31/anthropic_claude_code_source_code/).

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent article covering the Claude Code source leak with strong structure and good cross-references to the Mythos leak. Well-written analysis section.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources verified. The Register confirmed: source map, 1900 files, 512K lines, Shou discovery, 41.5K forks, Anhaia quote, Anthropic statement. Fortune confirmed: agentic harness, second lapse after Mythos, Paz quote, Capybara references. Raschka confirmed: git context loading, prompt cache, specialized tools (Grep/Glob/LSP), memory system, subagent architecture.

editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Appropriately notes the irony without editorializing. Good transparency about unknowns.
editor_notes.originality: Novel topic — Claude Code source leak is a breaking story from today. Good continuity with existing Mythos leak article.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Human-requested article meeting all editorial standards with heightened scrutiny applied. Three diverse sources verified. Ready for publication.
